title: Butter on Linen
stainChemistry: Butter is ~80% butterfat (saturated + monounsaturated triglycerides) + small milk casein fraction + salt + water. The protein fraction is small but heat-sets — cold water flush before warm Dawn. Linen fiber tolerates the standard butter protocol on simple flat pieces; structured / lined / dry-clean-only linen = pro. Linen-specific cautions apply: cool water (shrinks under hot), air-dry flat (heat shrinks + sets butterfat).
whyThisWorks: Cold water flush handles the casein fraction first. Dawn emulsifies butterfat (Dawn is engineered for this). Cool wash + air-dry preserves linen shape and prevents residual fat from polymerizing. Standard butter protocol.
defaultAssumption: Simple linen napkin / shirt = home protocol. Structured / lined / vintage = pro.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only / structured = pro. Simple flat linen = proceed. Scrape any solid butter with a dull blade.
Flip inside out. Cold water through the BACK. Cold flush handles the small casein fraction before warm Dawn.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + warm (not hot) water, soft toothbrush
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn in warm water (after cold flush handled casein). Soft toothbrush 5 min. Cool rinse.
Cool wash — NEVER hot (linen shrinks). AIR-DRY FLAT. Inspect before any heat.

steps: 4. Dawn + soft toothbrush warm water — fat step.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold flush + warm Dawn (not hot) + cool wash
warnings: NEVER tumble-dry until fully clear — heat shrinks + polymerizes residual fat
Cold flush before warm Dawn — casein step before fat step

homeSolutions.agent: Structured Linen Goes to Pro; Simple Linen Stays Home
homeSolutions.instruction: Linen has two cases. SIMPLE LINEN (napkin, basic shirt) = standard butter protocol. STRUCTURED LINEN (suits, lined dresses, dry-clean-only) = pro. Wet treatment risks shape change.
Scrape any solid butter off with a dull butter knife. Just lift the bulk.
Flip inside out. Cold tap water through the BACK 1 minute. Butter has a small milk-protein fraction (casein); cold flush handles it before warm water touches the fat.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Warm (Not Hot) Water + Soft Toothbrush
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix Dawn in WARM water — not hot, just warm enough to help emulsify butterfat. Work into spot with soft toothbrush 5 min. Cool rinse.
Cool wash per care label — NEVER hot (linen shrinks). AIR-DRY FLAT — never tumble-dry until fully clear (heat shrinks linen + polymerizes residual butterfat).

steps: 4. Dawn + WARM (not hot) water + soft toothbrush, 5 min.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if linen is structured / lined / trimmed / dry-clean-only.
warnings: NEVER tumble-dry until fully clear — heat shrinks + polymerizes butterfat
Cold flush BEFORE warm Dawn — handles casein first

neverDo: Never wet-treat structured linen at home
fiberSensitivities: Linen shrinks aggressively under hot water
Cool wash + air-dry flat preserves shape
Heat polymerizes residual butterfat into linen

materialWarnings: Cold flush handles small casein fraction first

escalation.when: Structured / lined / trimmed linen, hot water / dryer used previously, vintage piece, or polymerized residual butterfat after one home cycle.
escalation.whatToTell: "Butter on [simple / structured / vintage] linen, age [hours / days], home-treated with cold flush + Dawn warm water + cool wash + air-dry flat. No hot water / no dryer until clear. [Residual oily feel / structured concerns]."

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh / dried in / weeks?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Compound butter (garlic, herb) adds minor pigment that may need 3% peroxide step. Hot-dried butter polymerizes into a near-permanent stain on linen.
